manifest.name: GTM Pipeline Q2 Review Service Blueprint
manifest.readme:
# GTM Pipeline Q2 Review

Canonical GTM agent showcase package for governed Q2 pipeline review, enrichment, prioritization, routing previews, and outreach draft preparation.

This package contains the signed ANIP behavior contract, recommended lock file, and tooling metadata for agent integration.
Custom implementation code is not embedded in the behavior contract unless it is explicitly attached as implementation material.

Project: 85afbf8a-2414-49c1-824d-36d6d300b5f6
Package: gtm-pipeline-q2-review@0.4.3
Services: 4 (gtm-pipeline-service, gtm-enrichment-service, gtm-prioritization-service, gtm-outreach-service)
Capabilities: 23 (gtm.pipeline_summary, gtm.pipeline_forecast_summary, gtm.stage_bottleneck_summary, gtm.sales_team_performance_summary, gtm.product_pipeline_summary, gtm.stalled_opportunity_review, gtm.account_risk_summary, gtm.prepare_followup_tasks, gtm.prepare_reassignment_plan, gtm.at_risk_followup_preparation, gtm.at_risk_reassignment_preparation, gtm.account_enrichment_summary, ...)

## Generate

From a downloaded package bundle:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --package-bundle <downloaded-package>.anip-package.json --target python --dependency-source local --output ./generated/gtm-pipeline-q2-review --force
```

From a trusted registry package:

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-generate --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id gtm-pipeline-q2-review --package-version 0.4.3 --target python --dependency-source registry --output ./generated/gtm-pipeline-q2-review --force
```

## Verify

```bash
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --definition anip-service-definition.json
go run ./cmd/anip-verify --registry-url <registry-url> --package-id gtm-pipeline-q2-review --package-version 0.4.3
```

service_topology_bindings.implementation_notes:
Accepted assistant guidance:
- Formalize the capability surface each service must expose: Define the stable capability contracts each service is expected to own, including which actions are read-only, which require approval, and which must stop for clarification.
  Why: The generator and verifier need stable capability ownership, not only narrative service descriptions.
- Describe the required coordination edges between services: Capture where one service depends on another for bounded context, approvals, or downstream execution so coordination can be represented explicitly instead of as hidden glue.
  Why: Coordination edges are easier to validate when they are declared before implementation.
- Keep approval boundaries outside the main happy path: Identify which service should prepare a proposed action and which surface or role must approve it before the mutation boundary is crossed.
  Why: Approval semantics should be explicit in service design rather than buried in one implementation path.
- Preserve explanation and traceability across service boundaries: Define which service boundaries must preserve explanation, lineage, or durable evidence so later verification can reconstruct why a result or stop condition occurred.
  Why: Cross-service traceability should be deliberate, not inferred after implementation exists.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-oriented analytics, enrichment, and draft generation can return governed results directly when in scope. Operational next-step capabilities may prepare previews, but routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, and other write-adjacent actions must stop at approval_required, with authority-sensitive outcomes and durable reviewability.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product must answer recurring GTM questions through bounded, explainable capabilities with stable parameters and clear service ownership. It must clarify missing critical inputs instead of guessing, restrict or mask results when actor scope is limited, deny raw export or other out-of-scope requests, remain read-only or draft-only where specified, and preserve the same governed boundaries across multi-service compositions.

sections.description: How restrictions, denials, approvals, and clarification posture become formal service behavior.
sections.label: Authority & Approval

linked_product_design_items.detail: Read-oriented analytics, enrichment, and draft generation can return governed results directly when in scope. Operational next-step capabilities may prepare previews, but routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, and other write-adjacent actions must stop at approval_required, with authority-sensitive outcomes and durable reviewability.
linked_product_design_items.label: Approval Posture Summary
linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Approval Posture

linked_product_design_items.detail: May encounter approval-gated previews for write-adjacent actions. Final approval authority is not explicit in the brief and should be confirmed rather than assumed.
linked_product_design_items.label: Sales Leader approval expectations

linked_product_design_items.detail: Strong candidate for approval involvement on operational previews, but the brief does not explicitly assign final approval authority by action type; confirm before encoding approver status.

linked_product_design_items.detail: Should expect approval stops for write-adjacent preparation flows. The brief does not establish this actor as an approver and suggests lower-authority actors may be denied where higher-authority actors are allowed.

linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Business Goals

linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Business Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: Business goal the implementation should help achieve.
linked_product_design_items.label: Preserve Actor Aware Visibility, Masking, Clarification, Denial, Approval Gating, And Auditability Across Single Service And Multi Service Flows.
linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Business Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The product must answer recurring GTM questions through bounded, explainable capabilities with stable parameters and clear service ownership. It must clarify missing critical inputs instead of guessing, restrict or mask results when actor scope is limited, deny raw export or other out-of-scope requests, remain read-only or draft-only where specified, and preserve the same governed boundaries across multi-service compositions.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Behavior Summary
linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Governed Behavior

linked_product_design_items.detail: The umbrella and service specs repeatedly require bounded capabilities instead of unconstrained CRM, warehouse, BI, or query-language behavior.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase is not a generic "query anything in CRM or the warehouse" agent surface.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: Multiple source specs deny raw exports across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach surfaces.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not expose raw row-level CRM, enrichment, scoring, transcript, or training-data exports through the agent experience.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The source material consistently separates preparation from execution and requires approval stops before write-adjacent actions.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not execute routing, reassignment, follow-up creation, outreach sending, or other downstream mutations without explicit approval.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: Actor-specific visibility differences are a core governed behavior across the pipeline and umbrella specs.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not collapse actor-aware restriction, masking, and scope boundaries into one broad result for all users.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The umbrella spec explicitly preserves bounded service ownership and rejects one giant merged service or hidden prompt logic as the policy engine.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase is not one merged GTM super-service with hidden cross-service policy logic.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The umbrella spec explicitly rejects hidden prompt logic acting as the policy engine and requires reviewable governed behavior.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not rely on hidden prompt-only interpretation to decide governance, approvals, denials, or restrictions.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The source specs repeatedly state that behavior is representative, bounded, and not an exhaustive language or workflow inventory.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase is not trying to cover every GTM workflow or every possible user wording in the first release.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

sections.description: What evidence, lineage, and traceability the generated implementation must preserve.
sections.label: Audit & Lineage

linked_product_design_items.detail: Source business specs are visible in the project, derived PM and developer artifacts trace back to them, and runtime validation references the same governed capability surface.
linked_product_design_items.label: An informed reviewer can see that the GTM business intent was written first and that PM-facing requirements, developer design, and running service behavior derive from that source rather than hidden prompt logic.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative scenario review shows supported requests resolve through declared capability surfaces, while raw export, arbitrary query, and unsupported analysis requests are denied or constrained.
linked_product_design_items.label: The system answers recurring GTM questions through bounded capabilities with stable inputs and governed outcomes instead of generic CRM, BI, or raw export behavior.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative runs across pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and reassignment flows return usable governed outputs with bounded explanation and no unintended mutation.
linked_product_design_items.label: For in-scope requests, users receive explainable summaries, rankings, previews, or drafts that match the intended capability family and stay read-only or draft-only unless an approval-gated preview is explicitly allowed.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Runtime review of ambiguous scenarios shows clarification responses for missing required fields rather than inferred defaults.
linked_product_design_items.label: Missing critical inputs trigger explicit clarification instead of guessing, including quarter, account scope, cohort, routing target, persona, or outreach target when required by the capability.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Comparative actor review shows allowed, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes vary appropriately by actor identity and requested scope.
linked_product_design_items.label: Actor-aware restriction and masking remain visible in runtime behavior so that different actors can receive different allowed scope, field visibility, or outcomes for the same business question.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Preview scenarios return approval-gated outputs and do not execute writes; higher- and lower-authority actors show the expected differences in preparation versus denial.
linked_product_design_items.label: Operational preparation flows stop cleanly at approval_required before any routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, or other downstream mutation.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Cross-service scenarios show each step uses the canonical owning capability surface, with no hidden consumer-side policy substitution or merged super-service behavior.
linked_product_design_items.label: Service ownership and capability boundaries remain explicit across the showcase, including compound flows that span more than one service.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative multi-step flows demonstrate that governance survives service chaining rather than disappearing once orchestration begins.
linked_product_design_items.label: Compound workflows preserve the same governed behaviors as single-service calls, including clarification, denial, restriction, masking, and approval stops at the correct step in the chain.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Audit review for representative calls can explain both the final outcome and why different actors received different results.
linked_product_design_items.label: Every governed call is auditable enough to reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome was success, clarification, restriction, denial, or approval-required.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Local reproduction, Studio inspection, runtime metadata validation, and regression results all confirm the implementation still matches the intended bounded design.
linked_product_design_items.label: The same governed services can be reproduced locally, inspected in Studio, and validated end to end through runtime checks and regression-backed review across supported flows.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not execute routing, reassignment, follow-up creation, outreach sending, or other downstream mutations without explicit approval.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not rely on hidden prompt-only interpretation to decide governance, approvals, denials, or restrictions.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The source specs repeatedly state that behavior is representative, bounded, and not an exhaustive language or workflow inventory.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Source business specs are visible in the project, derived PM and developer artifacts trace back to them, and runtime validation references the same governed capability surface.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: An informed reviewer can see that the GTM business intent was written first and that PM-facing requirements, developer design, and running service behavior derive from that source rather than hidden prompt logic.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative scenario review shows supported requests resolve through declared capability surfaces, while raw export, arbitrary query, and unsupported analysis requests are denied or constrained.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The system answers recurring GTM questions through bounded capabilities with stable inputs and governed outcomes instead of generic CRM, BI, or raw export behavior.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative runs across pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and reassignment flows return usable governed outputs with bounded explanation and no unintended mutation.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: For in-scope requests, users receive explainable summaries, rankings, previews, or drafts that match the intended capability family and stay read-only or draft-only unless an approval-gated preview is explicitly allowed.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Runtime review of ambiguous scenarios shows clarification responses for missing required fields rather than inferred defaults.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Missing critical inputs trigger explicit clarification instead of guessing, including quarter, account scope, cohort, routing target, persona, or outreach target when required by the capability.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Comparative actor review shows allowed, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes vary appropriately by actor identity and requested scope.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Actor-aware restriction and masking remain visible in runtime behavior so that different actors can receive different allowed scope, field visibility, or outcomes for the same business question.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Preview scenarios return approval-gated outputs and do not execute writes; higher- and lower-authority actors show the expected differences in preparation versus denial.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Operational preparation flows stop cleanly at approval_required before any routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, or other downstream mutation.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Cross-service scenarios show each step uses the canonical owning capability surface, with no hidden consumer-side policy substitution or merged super-service behavior.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Service ownership and capability boundaries remain explicit across the showcase, including compound flows that span more than one service.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative multi-step flows demonstrate that governance survives service chaining rather than disappearing once orchestration begins.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Compound workflows preserve the same governed behaviors as single-service calls, including clarification, denial, restriction, masking, and approval stops at the correct step in the chain.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review for representative calls can explain both the final outcome and why different actors received different results.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Every governed call is auditable enough to reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome was success, clarification, restriction, denial, or approval-required.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Local reproduction, Studio inspection, runtime metadata validation, and regression results all confirm the implementation still matches the intended bounded design.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The same governed services can be reproduced locally, inspected in Studio, and validated end to end through runtime checks and regression-backed review across supported flows.

actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can review broad GTM summaries and cross-cutting business views, but still remains subject to actor-aware restriction and masking where the contract requires different visibility by actor.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can request bounded analytics, enrichment context, prioritization recommendations, routing previews, follow-up preparation, and reassignment previews within allowed scope.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Strong candidate for approval involvement on operational previews, but the brief does not explicitly assign final approval authority by action type; confirm before encoding approver status.

linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Business Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The product must answer recurring GTM questions through bounded, explainable capabilities with stable parameters and clear service ownership. It must clarify missing critical inputs instead of guessing, restrict or mask results when actor scope is limited, deny raw export or other out-of-scope requests, remain read-only or draft-only where specified, and preserve the same governed boundaries across multi-service compositions.
linked_product_design_items.label: Governed Behavior Summary
linked_product_design_items.section: Business Summary · Governed Behavior

linked_product_design_items.detail: The umbrella and service specs repeatedly require bounded capabilities instead of unconstrained CRM, warehouse, BI, or query-language behavior.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase is not a generic "query anything in CRM or the warehouse" agent surface.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: Multiple source specs deny raw exports across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach surfaces.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not expose raw row-level CRM, enrichment, scoring, transcript, or training-data exports through the agent experience.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The source material consistently separates preparation from execution and requires approval stops before write-adjacent actions.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not execute routing, reassignment, follow-up creation, outreach sending, or other downstream mutations without explicit approval.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: Actor-specific visibility differences are a core governed behavior across the pipeline and umbrella specs.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not collapse actor-aware restriction, masking, and scope boundaries into one broad result for all users.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The umbrella spec explicitly preserves bounded service ownership and rejects one giant merged service or hidden prompt logic as the policy engine.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase is not one merged GTM super-service with hidden cross-service policy logic.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The umbrella spec explicitly rejects hidden prompt logic acting as the policy engine and requires reviewable governed behavior.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase does not rely on hidden prompt-only interpretation to decide governance, approvals, denials, or restrictions.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

linked_product_design_items.detail: The source specs repeatedly state that behavior is representative, bounded, and not an exhaustive language or workflow inventory.
linked_product_design_items.label: This showcase is not trying to cover every GTM workflow or every possible user wording in the first release.
linked_product_design_items.section: Non-Goals

sections.description: How the ANIP surface maps to real backend systems, adapters, operations, and implementation targets.
sections.label: Runtime Backends

sections.description: What evidence, lineage, and traceability the generated implementation must preserve.
sections.label: Audit & Lineage

linked_product_design_items.detail: Source business specs are visible in the project, derived PM and developer artifacts trace back to them, and runtime validation references the same governed capability surface.
linked_product_design_items.label: An informed reviewer can see that the GTM business intent was written first and that PM-facing requirements, developer design, and running service behavior derive from that source rather than hidden prompt logic.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative scenario review shows supported requests resolve through declared capability surfaces, while raw export, arbitrary query, and unsupported analysis requests are denied or constrained.
linked_product_design_items.label: The system answers recurring GTM questions through bounded capabilities with stable inputs and governed outcomes instead of generic CRM, BI, or raw export behavior.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative runs across pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and reassignment flows return usable governed outputs with bounded explanation and no unintended mutation.
linked_product_design_items.label: For in-scope requests, users receive explainable summaries, rankings, previews, or drafts that match the intended capability family and stay read-only or draft-only unless an approval-gated preview is explicitly allowed.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Runtime review of ambiguous scenarios shows clarification responses for missing required fields rather than inferred defaults.
linked_product_design_items.label: Missing critical inputs trigger explicit clarification instead of guessing, including quarter, account scope, cohort, routing target, persona, or outreach target when required by the capability.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Comparative actor review shows allowed, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes vary appropriately by actor identity and requested scope.
linked_product_design_items.label: Actor-aware restriction and masking remain visible in runtime behavior so that different actors can receive different allowed scope, field visibility, or outcomes for the same business question.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Preview scenarios return approval-gated outputs and do not execute writes; higher- and lower-authority actors show the expected differences in preparation versus denial.
linked_product_design_items.label: Operational preparation flows stop cleanly at approval_required before any routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, or other downstream mutation.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Cross-service scenarios show each step uses the canonical owning capability surface, with no hidden consumer-side policy substitution or merged super-service behavior.
linked_product_design_items.label: Service ownership and capability boundaries remain explicit across the showcase, including compound flows that span more than one service.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Representative multi-step flows demonstrate that governance survives service chaining rather than disappearing once orchestration begins.
linked_product_design_items.label: Compound workflows preserve the same governed behaviors as single-service calls, including clarification, denial, restriction, masking, and approval stops at the correct step in the chain.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Audit review for representative calls can explain both the final outcome and why different actors received different results.
linked_product_design_items.label: Every governed call is auditable enough to reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome was success, clarification, restriction, denial, or approval-required.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

linked_product_design_items.detail: Local reproduction, Studio inspection, runtime metadata validation, and regression results all confirm the implementation still matches the intended bounded design.
linked_product_design_items.label: The same governed services can be reproduced locally, inspected in Studio, and validated end to end through runtime checks and regression-backed review across supported flows.
linked_product_design_items.section: Success Criteria

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The umbrella and service specs repeatedly require bounded capabilities instead of unconstrained CRM, warehouse, BI, or query-language behavior.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase is not a generic "query anything in CRM or the warehouse" agent surface.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Multiple source specs deny raw exports across pipeline, enrichment, prioritization, and outreach surfaces.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not expose raw row-level CRM, enrichment, scoring, transcript, or training-data exports through the agent experience.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The source material consistently separates preparation from execution and requires approval stops before write-adjacent actions.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not execute routing, reassignment, follow-up creation, outreach sending, or other downstream mutations without explicit approval.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: Actor-specific visibility differences are a core governed behavior across the pipeline and umbrella specs.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not collapse actor-aware restriction, masking, and scope boundaries into one broad result for all users.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The umbrella spec explicitly preserves bounded service ownership and rejects one giant merged service or hidden prompt logic as the policy engine.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase is not one merged GTM super-service with hidden cross-service policy logic.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The umbrella spec explicitly rejects hidden prompt logic acting as the policy engine and requires reviewable governed behavior.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase does not rely on hidden prompt-only interpretation to decide governance, approvals, denials, or restrictions.

non_goal_guards.evidence_signal: The source specs repeatedly state that behavior is representative, bounded, and not an exhaustive language or workflow inventory.
non_goal_guards.guard_strategy: Preserve this non-goal as an explicit denial, restriction, clarification, or safe-stop rule where requests would otherwise cross the declared boundary.
non_goal_guards.non_goal: This showcase is not trying to cover every GTM workflow or every possible user wording in the first release.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Source business specs are visible in the project, derived PM and developer artifacts trace back to them, and runtime validation references the same governed capability surface.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: An informed reviewer can see that the GTM business intent was written first and that PM-facing requirements, developer design, and running service behavior derive from that source rather than hidden prompt logic.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative scenario review shows supported requests resolve through declared capability surfaces, while raw export, arbitrary query, and unsupported analysis requests are denied or constrained.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The system answers recurring GTM questions through bounded capabilities with stable inputs and governed outcomes instead of generic CRM, BI, or raw export behavior.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative runs across pipeline, forecast, bottleneck, team, product, enrichment, prioritization, outreach, and reassignment flows return usable governed outputs with bounded explanation and no unintended mutation.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: For in-scope requests, users receive explainable summaries, rankings, previews, or drafts that match the intended capability family and stay read-only or draft-only unless an approval-gated preview is explicitly allowed.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Runtime review of ambiguous scenarios shows clarification responses for missing required fields rather than inferred defaults.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Missing critical inputs trigger explicit clarification instead of guessing, including quarter, account scope, cohort, routing target, persona, or outreach target when required by the capability.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Comparative actor review shows allowed, masked, restricted, or denied outcomes vary appropriately by actor identity and requested scope.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Actor-aware restriction and masking remain visible in runtime behavior so that different actors can receive different allowed scope, field visibility, or outcomes for the same business question.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Preview scenarios return approval-gated outputs and do not execute writes; higher- and lower-authority actors show the expected differences in preparation versus denial.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Operational preparation flows stop cleanly at approval_required before any routing, reassignment, follow-up execution, or other downstream mutation.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Cross-service scenarios show each step uses the canonical owning capability surface, with no hidden consumer-side policy substitution or merged super-service behavior.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Service ownership and capability boundaries remain explicit across the showcase, including compound flows that span more than one service.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Representative multi-step flows demonstrate that governance survives service chaining rather than disappearing once orchestration begins.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Compound workflows preserve the same governed behaviors as single-service calls, including clarification, denial, restriction, masking, and approval stops at the correct step in the chain.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Audit review for representative calls can explain both the final outcome and why different actors received different results.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Every governed call is auditable enough to reconstruct who asked, which actor identity applied, which capability ran, which normalized parameters were used, and why the outcome was success, clarification, restriction, denial, or approval-required.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Local reproduction, Studio inspection, runtime metadata validation, and regression results all confirm the implementation still matches the intended bounded design.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: The same governed services can be reproduced locally, inspected in Studio, and validated end to end through runtime checks and regression-backed review across supported flows.
